In this video, we will explore the career history of Michael Wirth, from his early days as an engineer to his rise as CEO. We will also examine his mindset, principles, and key factors that he brings to Chevron. Finally, we will look at his influence on the industry and how he is shaping the future of energy. snippet.title: Chevron modest oil production increase in Venezuela.
snippet.description:
The balance between OFAC License 41 and conditions in the ground. 
Last December, I highlighted the modest effect of the recent OFAC special license awarded to Chevron related to its activities in Venezuela, in an interview for TRT World. Yesterday, Michael Wirth, Chevron CEO, said "...the recent shift in U.S. sanctions on Venezuela allowed its local joint venture to raise short-term production in the country by 50,000 barrels of oil per day (bpd) to 90,000 bpd." Wirth said in a webcast with analysts, adding that he couldn't say that further production trends could be extrapolated from that. Link: https://lnkd.in/g_YbWm6A
My full interview: https://lnkd.in/g3U8AmRi

snippet.title: Interview with oboist Ava Wirth
snippet.description:
Ava Wirth is a Chicago based oboist with a focus on contemporary music.  

As an avid supporter of contemporary music, Ava has taken part in commissions and recordings that promote new works. In 2018, she was principal oboist on the BGSU Wind Symphony CD, Trends: New Music for Wind Band, Vol. 1. Her most recent commissions and collaborations include Adam Kennaugh’s 2020 work for solo oboe, You’re Here Because Someone Won a Fight With a Rock and Josh Trentadue’s 2021 piece for five-part ensemble, Meditation and Hymn to Nature, which was commissioned by her ensemble, The _____ Experiment.

snippet.title: BC CEO Club: John Stankey, CEO of AT&T
snippet.description:
The Boston College Chief Executives Club welcomed John Stankey, CEO of AT&T. John Stankey engaged in a fireside chat with Marianne Harrison, President & CEO of John Hancock.

The conversation focused on AT&T’s investment in infrastructure, the impact of climate control on their business, their investment into secure networks for first responders, and the company’s investment on connecting all areas of the country to their network
